Next industry: enterprise (index 11). KEY FINDS THIS RUN (energy): (1) OpenEMS (EPL-2.0, 1.1k+ stars) — Modular Java EMS for BESS/EV charging/heat pumps/PV. 50k+ deployed sites in Europe. REST API + websocket. THE production EMS platform — 50k deployments means proven production robustness. Foundation for any BESS or microgrid engagement. (2) MyEMS (MIT, 1.5k+ stars) — Full-stack Python/React EMS. ISO 50001-ready, carbon reporting, VPP + PV modules, AI optimization layer. Docker-compose ready. The open-source alternative to Schneider EcoStruxure — MIT license means zero lock-in. (3) PyPSA (MIT, 2.0k+ stars) — Python for Power System Analysis. OPF, investment optimization, hydrogen networks, sector coupling. IRENA/KTH use it for national energy planning. Lingua franca for energy system modeling. Wrap as optimization microservice and LangGraph agents call network.optimize(). (4) OpenSTEF (MPL-2.0, 400+ stars) — Open Short-Term Energy Forecasting by Alliander DSO. LF Energy project. AutoML (XGBoost/LightGBM/LassoCV) for probabilistic grid load forecasts. Production at 500+ substations. THIS IS ALREADY THE AI AGENT. Add Claude for operator report narration. (5) pandapower (BSD-3, 1.2k+ stars) — Power system modeling: AC/DC load flow, OPF, short circuit, state estimation. 100+ utilities worldwide. (6) OSeMOSYS (Apache-2.0, 200+ stars) — Open Source Energy Modelling System. Long-term national energy planning with hydrogen/storage/sector coupling. KTH/IRENA/UN standard in 30+ countries. (7) PyPSA-Earth (MIT, 340+ stars) — Global open energy system model. Model ANY country's energy future using OSM + ERA5 weather. Brazilian and Chilean energy agencies adopting for national planning. (8) power-grid-model (MPL-2.0, 180+ stars) — LF Energy C++/Python: ultra-fast distribution grid power flow. 2M nodes/second. Used by Alliander in production. (9) entsoe-py (MIT, 600+ stars) — Python client for ENTSO-E API. Day-ahead prices, generation mix, cross-border flows. European energy market data layer. (10) GridLAB-D (BSD-3, 210+ stars) — PNNL/DOE distribution grid simulator. DER integration, 20+ years institutional use. MARKET: AI in Energy $21B (2025) to $42B (2026), 17-33% CAGR.
